principle: Codex, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Pro, Azure AI Foundry, Azure CLI, Hermes, XCore, local models, and HELIOS agents share one C# safety/orchestration core, one policy/audit layer, one learning event store, and language-specialized engines for performance, analytics, provider glue, and UI reliability.

coreDna.identity: one agent/model/task ontology across local and cloud providers
coreDna.safety: policy gate, audit trail, secret-name-only readiness, and disabled mutating actions until explicit apply gates pass
coreDna.learning: all providers and agents emit comparable success, failure, cost, latency, quality, and risk events
coreDna.optimization: route each task to the cheapest fast-enough safe-enough model/agent/language combination, then promote repeated hot paths into C++ or F# and stable orchestration into C#

providerFamilies.role: codebase editing, branch tasks, local agent handoffs, test/fix loops
planning, report summarization, tool-using agent calls, dashboard explanations, handoff packets
developer IDE assistance and PR/code suggestions
Teams/SharePoint/OneDrive/M365 context surfaces, operator summaries, enterprise knowledge connectors
managed model deployments, evaluation, prompt/model operations, cloud-hosted AI workflows
specialized local/cloud models, offline fallback, cost-controlled batch work, domain agents

routingHeuristics: Use C# to coordinate provider calls, approvals, typed contracts, UI state, and report validation.
Use F# to rank agents/models/branches and learn from unitary provider telemetry.
Use C++ when telemetry shows repeated hot paths in diffing, indexing, rendering, vector math, or scanning.
Use Python for provider SDKs, AIHub, Linux runner glue, OpenAI/Hermes/XCore adapters, and mature AI/ML libraries.
Use Azure CLI/Bicep for cloud state changes only after what-if, policy, vault, and audit gates pass.
Treat Codex, ChatGPT, Copilot, M365 Copilot, Copilot Pro, Azure Foundry, Hermes, XCore, and local models as interchangeable providers behind the same learning/audit schema, not as separate silos.
deepSetupOrder: agent XP, party, fleet deployment, and learning summary
